# nerdctl_exec (Action)

Runs a command in an existing container with `nerdctl exec`. Output is emitted as action progress; it cannot be captured into state. No TTY is allocated.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nerdctl_container" "app" {
  name  = "app"
  image = "nginx:alpine"

  lifecycle {
    action_trigger {
      events  = [after_create]
      actions = [action.nerdctl_exec.mark_ready]
    }
  }
}

action "nerdctl_exec" "mark_ready" {
  config {
    container = nerdctl_container.app.name
    command   = ["sh", "-c", "echo ready > /usr/share/nginx/html/status"]
  }
}
```

<!-- action schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `command` (List of String) Command and arguments to execute.
- `container` (String) Container name or ID to execute in.

### Optional

- `detach` (Boolean) Run the command in the background and return immediately.
- `env` (List of String) Environment variables for the command, as `KEY=value` entries.
- `privileged` (Boolean) Give extended privileges to the command.
- `user` (String) User to run as, `user[:group]` by name or ID.
- `workdir` (String) Working directory inside the container.
